articles.title: LIVE: US–Iran War Enters Third Week
articles.description: Tensions in the Middle East continue to escalate as the conflict between the United States and Iran enters its third week. Military strikes and drone attacks threaten global oil routes through the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: French Municipal Elections 2026: First Results Announced
articles.description: France held the first round of nationwide municipal elections with close races in major cities including Paris, Marseille and Lyon. Many municipalities will move to a second round vote scheduled for March 22.

articles.title: Flights Disrupted Worldwide Due to Middle East Conflict
articles.description: Airlines across Europe, Asia and the Gulf have rerouted flights to avoid conflict zones. Aviation analysts warn that longer routes and higher fuel costs may increase global air travel prices.

articles.title: Formula One Cancels Bahrain and Saudi Arabian Grand Prix
articles.description: Formula One officials confirmed the cancellation of the Bahrain and Saudi Arabian races scheduled for April due to regional security concerns linked to the Middle East conflict.

articles.title: Spain vs Argentina Finalissima Match Cancelled
articles.description: The highly anticipated football match between Spain and Argentina scheduled in Qatar has been cancelled amid regional tensions and travel disruptions.

articles.title: Scientists Investigate Death of 150 Sea Turtles
articles.description: Marine scientists are investigating the discovery of nearly 150 dead sea turtles. Experts suspect pollution, fishing equipment entanglement or climate changes in marine ecosystems.

articles.title: Prince Harry Biography Sparks Global Debate
articles.description: A newly released biography about Prince Harry has sparked controversy after the duke's spokesperson dismissed claims in the book as a conspiracy theory.

articles.title: Nigerian Music Star CKay Expands Afro-Fusion Sound
articles.description: Afrobeats singer CKay says his upcoming music projects will explore new styles of Afro-fusion blending emotional pop with African rhythms.

articles.title: Nigerian Student Dies After Plasma Donation in Canada
articles.description: Authorities are investigating the death of a 22-year-old Nigerian student after complications linked to plasma donation procedures at a Canadian clinic.

articles.title: Lagos Resumes Monthly Environmental Sanitation
articles.description: Authorities in Lagos State announced the return of the monthly environmental sanitation exercise beginning April 25 to improve public health and waste management.

articles.title: Oil Prices Rise as Global Markets React to Middle East War
articles.description: Energy markets reacted strongly to the conflict in the Middle East with oil prices climbing amid fears of supply disruptions.

articles.title: NASA Clears Four Astronauts for Moon Mission
articles.description: NASA has approved four astronauts for an upcoming lunar mission as part of the global effort to expand human exploration of the Moon.

articles.title: Middle East War Expands Global Tensions
articles.description: As the war between the U.S. and Iran enters its third week, attention has increasingly turned to senior military leaders directing the campaign. The Strait of Hormuz has become a major focal point amid missile strikes and drone attacks.

articles.title: Formula 1 Cancels Bahrain and Saudi Races
articles.description: Formula One officials announced that the Bahrain Grand Prix and Saudi Arabian Grand Prix scheduled for April have been cancelled due to security concerns linked to the Middle East conflict.

articles.title: Explosion Targets Jewish School in Amsterdam
articles.description: Authorities in Amsterdam are investigating an explosion at a Jewish school building, described as a 'targeted attack'. Security has been increased around synagogues and schools.

articles.title: U.S. Senator Bill Cassidy Faces Tough Reelection Battle
articles.description: Bill Cassidy, Republican senator from Louisiana, faces opposition from factions aligned with the 'Make America Healthy Again' movement, highlighting divisions within conservative politics ahead of upcoming elections.

articles.title: NDLEA Arrests Elderly Drug Suspect at Abuja Airport
articles.description: Nigeria’s anti-drug agency arrested an elderly man at Nnamdi Azikiwe International Airport after cocaine was allegedly discovered hidden inside packaged pepper.

articles.title: CKay Aims to Redefine Afro-Fusion Music
articles.description: Nigerian singer CKay says his upcoming projects will push the boundaries of Afro-fusion by blending Afrobeats with emotional pop influences, expanding the global reach of African music.

articles.title: Gospel Music Mourns Veteran Singer Toun Soetan
articles.description: Nigeria’s gospel music community is mourning the death of veteran singer Toun Soetan, who passed away at the age of 73, leaving a legacy spanning decades.

articles.title: Lagos Resumes Monthly Environmental Sanitation
articles.description: Authorities in Lagos announced that the state's monthly environmental sanitation exercise will resume on April 25, part of efforts to improve public health and waste management.

articles.title: PalmPay Expands Operations Across Nigeria
articles.description: Financial technology company PalmPay announced plans to expand offices across Nigeria to increase financial inclusion and bring digital payment services to more underserved communities.

articles.title: NASA Approves Four Astronauts for Moon Mission
articles.description: Following a unanimous risk-assessment review, NASA approved the launch of four astronauts for an upcoming mission to the Moon, representing another step in long-term lunar exploration.

articles.title: French Municipales 2026 Results: Macron’s Allies Gain Ground
articles.description: Preliminary results from the 2026 French municipal elections show that President Emmanuel Macron’s centrist allies have gained key municipalities, while opposition parties make incremental progress in urban centers.

articles.title: Travelers Stranded as Middle East Conflict Disrupts Flights
articles.description: Airlines across Europe, Asia, and the Gulf rerouted flights to avoid conflict zones, causing widespread delays and cancellations, leaving travelers stranded in multiple countries.

articles.title: Michigan Synagogue Attack Sparks Security Alarm
articles.description: Authorities are investigating a violent incident at Temple Israel synagogue, where a man rammed a truck into the building before opening fire, raising concerns about safety at places of worship across the United States.

articles.title: Italian Citizenship Ruling Affects Millions Worldwide
articles.description: A new court interpretation in Italy could affect millions of people seeking citizenship through ancestry, potentially restricting eligibility for descendants abroad.

articles.title: Oscar-Nominated Films Return in Special Screenings
articles.description: Several Academy Awards nominated films are returning to theaters in immersive screenings and extended versions designed to bring audiences closer to the filmmaking experience.

articles.title: United States Advances in World Baseball Classic
articles.description: The United States defeated Canada 5–3 to reach the semifinals of the World Baseball Classic, keeping the defending champions on track for another strong tournament finish.

articles.title: Four Astronauts Cleared for Moon Mission
articles.description: NASA has cleared four astronauts for the upcoming lunar mission, marking another step in humanity’s renewed push for long-term exploration of the Moon and future missions to Mars.

articles.title: Nigerian Student Dies After Plasma Donation in Canada
articles.description: A 22-year-old Nigerian student reportedly died in Canada after complications linked to plasma donation procedures, sparking calls for closer monitoring of private plasma clinics.

articles.title: Government and Civil Society Groups Push Inclusive Healthcare
articles.description: Nigeria’s federal government partnered with civil society organizations to improve healthcare policies and expand access to medical services for vulnerable populations.
